name: Adyen API
what_it_does: Enterprise global payment platform providing end-to-end acquiring, payment processing, risk management, and financial services for large-scale merchants across online, in-store, and mobile channels.
use_cases: Processing global payments with unified acquiring across 150+ currencies
Marketplace and platform payment splits using Adyen for Platforms
Agent-driven payment authorization, capture, and refund workflows
Risk and fraud scoring via RevenueProtect as part of payment pipelines
Unified commerce integrations linking online and in-store payment data
not_for: Small businesses or startups (Adyen requires substantial transaction volume and enterprise contract)
Developers who need a simple sandbox to prototype quickly (onboarding takes weeks)
best_when: You're a large-scale merchant or marketplace processing high payment volumes globally and need a single payment platform that handles acquiring, risk, and reconciliation.
avoid_when: You're a small business, startup, or low-volume merchant — Stripe or Square will be far easier to work with.

auth.notes: API key for server-side calls (X-API-Key header). Client key for client-side (browser/mobile) Drop-in and Components. Role-based API credentials managed in the Customer Area. No OAuth — all keys are long-lived.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Sandbox environment is free but requires approved account
pricing.paid_tiers: Processing fees plus monthly fees; interchange-plus pricing for enterprise; not publicly disclosed
pricing.notes: Enterprise contracts with volume-based pricing. Not self-serve — requires account approval and contract. Typically competitive with Stripe for high-volume enterprise merchants.

performance.rate_limits: High throughput for enterprise accounts; specific limits negotiated per contract

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Response codes include resultCode, refusalReason, and refusalReasonCode for declines. Error objects contain errorCode, message, and errorType. Very comprehensive for payment operations.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Idempotency supported via reference field on payment requests. Same reference returns the original result without processing again. Essential for agent retry safety.
known_agent_gotchas: Account onboarding requires manual approval by Adyen — no self-serve sandbox for new accounts
Webhook signatures must be validated using HMAC-SHA256 — never trust unverified webhooks
Payment reference must be unique per transaction — duplicate references return the original result (idempotent)
Adyen has separate APIs per product area (Checkout API, Management API, Reporting API) — must know which to use
LIVE environment requires separate API credentials and different endpoint URLs than TEST
Currency amounts are in minor units (cents) — sending incorrect unit causes overpayment or underpayment

security_score_components.security_notes: API key + HMAC webhook signatures. Role-based access with specific user roles per API endpoint. PCI DSS Level 1 certified. IDEMPOTENCY built in for financial transactions. Adyen's enterprise focus means security is very mature.
